retry_reason: Put operations should never be retried; after dropping the reference the object may be freed, and retrying would cause a double-put (use-after-free).
contract_summary: This is a put wrapper that drops the backref node's reference on its extent buffer. It unlocks the buffer (if needed) and calls free_extent_buffer, which decrements the refcount and may free the buffer when it reaches zero. After calling this function, the caller must not use node->eb. It is safe to call when node->eb is NULL (no-op). This function is typically invoked when finishing processing of a backref node.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to release a reference on the extent buffer (node->eb) via free_extent_buffer, after unlocking it; the only side effect is setting node->eb to NULL. There is no other business logic.

check.reason: The function calls free_extent_buffer(node->eb), which is a put operation on the extent_buffer. The access path node.eb.refs.refs correctly traces from the function parameter node through the eb field to the refcount field refs.refs. The segment count matches the type chain.
